content.text: Below is the memory protection analysis
All Memory and Protection errors are reported as Traps in Aurix.
For all use cases when protection hook is called below information has to be logged

items: Program Status Word (Very important) - Os_GetExceptionContext (&ExceptionContext) -> This API can be used only in ProtectionHook API context

rows: Remove ALM 7[20] NMI config in SMU
Implement SEH in Exception handle as per design Remove FEL call in OsHooks_Callout_Shutdown_ErrorHandling and replace with SSH call
Implement SEH in Exception handle as per design
interrupt requests from unassigned interrupt sources
Get the unhandled Interrupt from Os_GetUnhandledIrq and log it properly
Get the serviceId OSErrorGetServiceId and log it properly
Get the unhandled Interrupt Os_GetUnhandledIrq and log it properly
Instruction which can be executed only in Supervisor rights is executed in user mode
Interestingly this ends up in E_OS_PROTECTION_MEMORY from OS

inconsistent OS internal state Unhandled interrupt request in User critical section Unhandled syscall request which has occurred within OS code Unhandled exception request which has occurred within OS code Memory protection violation exception which has occurred within critical user sections
We cannot use GetTaskId and GetIsrId, Os_GetExceptionContext inside Panchook But we can get exception address Os_GetExceptionAddress And we can read exception source (TIN) from D[15] register

content.text: In case of a severe error the PanicHook might not be called in all cases

Before calling the PanicHook, the OS tries to disable all interrupts. If the OS is not in supervisor mode, a class 1 TIN 1 trap (privileged instruction) occurs. The OS will initiate protection violation handling and the ProtectionHook will be called instead of the PanicHook
